semanticCore: Frowning, distress, intensity of calamity, severe day, or binding together

keyDerivations.meaning: Distressful, calamitous, frown-inducing, intensely severe, or long day

figurativeOrRareUsages: إِنَّا نَخَافُ مِنْ رَبِّنَا يَوْمًا عَبُوسًا قَمْطَرِيرًا (76:10): 'Indeed, we fear from our Lord a day frown-inducing and distressful/intense.'
discardedNoise: Qamtary (book binder box) - classical library term.
summaryEn: The root refers to an intensely severe, frowning day (Qamtarir), conveying the dread believers have of the Day of Judgment's distressful atmosphere.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى العبوس الشديد وتوالي الشرور وضيق النفوس وشدة بلاء اليوم (قمطر)، واصفاً أهوال يوم القيامة باليوم العبوس القمطرير المشحون بالشدة.
rawText: إِقْمَطَرَّ It (a day, S) was, or became, distressful, or calamitous. (S, K.) قمطر قَمْطَرٌ : see قَمْطَرِيرٌ. قُمَاطِرٌ : see قَمْطَرِيرٌ, in two places. قِمَطْرٌ and قِمَطْرَةٌ (S, Msb, K) and with tesh-deed, [i. e. قِمَّطْرٌ and قِمَّطْرَةٌ,] but this pronunciation is extr., (K,) or, accord. to Yaakoob, (S,) or ISk, (TA,) not allowable, (S, TA,) A repository for books or writings, (S, Msb, K, TA,) resembling a سَفَط, [q. v., (in the TA, سقط, which is evidently a mistake,)] made of reeds woven together: (TA:) the first word is fem., like the second, as well as masc.: (Msb:) pl. قَمَاطِرُ. (S, Msb.) مُقْمَطِرٌّ : see قَمْطَرِيرٌ, in two places. يَوْمٌ قَمْطَرِيرٌ , and 🡻قُمَاطِرٌ, (S, K,) and 🡻مُقْمَطِرٌّ, (TA,) A distressful, or calamitous, day: (S, K:) or a day that makes one knit the brow, or contract the skin between the eyes: so the first is explained by some as occurring in the Kur lxxvi. 10. (TA.) === شَرٌّ قَمْطَرِيرٌ, (TA,) and 🡻قُمَاطِرٌ, and 🡻قَمْطَرٌ, (Lth, TA,) and 🡻مُقْمَطِرٌّ, (TA,) Intense evil. (Lth, TA.)
